-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Tracing
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Trace events for MCP interface debugging.
data MCPTrace
    = MCPTrace_Initialize Text
    | MCPTrace_ToolsListed Int
    | MCPTrace_ToolCalled Text Text -- tool name, conversation id
    | MCPTrace_ToolCompleted Text (Either String Text)
    | MCPTrace_Error Text
    | MCPTrace_SessionProgress ConversationId Text
    deriving (Show)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Interface Configuration
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Persistence configuration for MCP sessions.
data MCPPersistence
    = -- | No session persistence
      MCPPersistence_None
    | -- | Persist to SQLite database
      MCPPersistence_Sqlite FilePath
    | -- | Custom persistence callback
      MCPPersistence_Custom (ConversationId -> Session -> IO ())
    deriving (Show)

-- | Configuration for the MCP interface.
data MCPInterfaceConfig = MCPInterfaceConfig
    { mcpBaseConfig :: InterfaceConfig
    -- ^ Base interface configuration
    , mcpProtocolVersion :: Text
    -- ^ MCP protocol version (default: "2024-11-05")
    , mcpServerName :: Text
    -- ^ Server name for initialize response
    , mcpServerVersion :: Text
    -- ^ Server version for initialize response
    , mcpPersistence :: MCPPersistence
    -- ^ Session persistence configuration
    , mcpOnSessionProgress :: OnSessionProgress
    -- ^ Callback for session progress tracking
    , mcpTracer :: Tracer IO MCPTrace
    -- ^ Tracer for debugging
    }

-- | Default MCP interface configuration.
defaultMCPInterfaceConfig :: IO MCPInterfaceConfig
defaultMCPInterfaceConfig = do
    let tracer = Tracer $ \_ -> pure () -- silent by default
    pure $
        MCPInterfaceConfig
            { mcpBaseConfig = (defaultInterfaceConfig){ifcMode = ModeMCP}
            , mcpProtocolVersion = "2024-11-05"
            , mcpServerName = "agents-exe-mcp-server"
            , mcpServerVersion = "0.1.0"
            , mcpPersistence = MCPPersistence_None
            , mcpOnSessionProgress = ignoreSessionProgress
            , mcpTracer = tracer
            }

-- | Operating mode (internal use).
data InterfaceMode = ModeMCP
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Mapped Tools
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | A mapped tool representing an expert agent exposed as an MCP tool.
Stores the OSAgentTree along with the API keys needed for execution.
-}
data MappedTool = MappedTool
    { mappedToolName :: Mcp.Name
    -- ^ MCP tool name (derived from agent slug)
    , mappedToolAgentId :: AgentId
    -- ^ OS entity ID for the agent
    , mappedToolTree :: AgentTree.OSAgentTree
    -- ^ The agent tree with configuration
    , mappedToolApiKeys :: AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys
    -- ^ API keys for LLM calls
    }
    deriving (Show)

type MappedTools = [MappedTool]

-- | Register an agent tree as an MCP tool.
registerAgentAsTool :: AgentTree.OSAgentTree -> AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys -> IO MappedTool
registerAgentAsTool tree apiKeys = do
    let node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
    let agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
    let toolName = agentCfg.slug
    agentId <- newAgentId
    pure $
        MappedTool
            { mappedToolName = toolName
            , mappedToolAgentId = agentId
            , mappedToolTree = tree
            , mappedToolApiKeys = apiKeys
            }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Runtime
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | MCP server runtime state.
data MCPRuntime = MCPRuntime
    { mcpRuntimeTools :: TVar MappedTools
    -- ^ Registered tools
    , mcpRuntimeSessions :: TVar (Map ConversationId Session)
    -- ^ Active sessions
    , mcpRuntimePendingRequests :: TVar [(Rpc.Request, Async ())]
    -- ^ Pending async requests for cancellation
    , mcpRuntimePersistence :: Maybe PersistenceHandle
    -- ^ Optional persistence handle
    , mcpRuntimeOnProgress :: OnSessionProgress
    -- ^ Session progress callback
    , mcpRuntimeTracer :: Tracer IO MCPTrace
    -- ^ Tracer for debugging
    }

-- | MCP server monad stack.
newtype McpStack a = McpStack {runMcpStack :: ReaderT MCPRuntime IO a}
    deriving (Functor, Applicative, Monad, MonadIO, MonadReader MCPRuntime)

instance MonadLogger McpStack where
    monadLoggerLog _ _ _ msg = do
        liftIO $ putStrLn $ Text.unpack msg

instance MonadLoggerIO McpStack where
    askLoggerIO = pure $ \_ _ _ msg -> putStrLn $ Text.unpack msg

-- | Get runtime from stack.
askRuntime :: Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack -> McpStack MCPRuntime
askRuntime _ = ask

-- | Get mapped tools.
askMappedTools :: McpStack MappedTools
askMappedTools = do
    rt <- ask
    readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)

-- | Add async request to pending list.
addAsyncRequest :: Rpc.Request -> Async () -> McpStack ()
addAsyncRequest req asyncHandle = do
    rt <- ask
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt) ((req, asyncHandle) :)

-- | Cancel pending request by ID.
cancelRequest :: Mcp.RequestId -> McpStack ()
cancelRequest reqId = do
    rt <- ask
    pending <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt)
    case find (matchingReqId reqId . fst) pending of
        Just (_, asyncHandle) -> do
            liftIO $ cancel asyncHandle
            atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt) (filter (not . matchingReqId reqId . fst))
        Nothing -> pure ()
  where
    matchingReqId :: Mcp.RequestId -> Rpc.Request -> Bool
    matchingReqId (Mcp.IntRequestId n) r = case r.getReqId of
        Rpc.IdInt m -> n == m
        _ -> False
    matchingReqId (Mcp.TextRequestId t) r = case r.getReqId of
        Rpc.IdTxt s -> t == s
        _ -> False

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Interface Handle
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Handle for MCP interface operations.
data MCPInterfaceHandle = MCPInterfaceHandle
    { mcpBaseHandle :: InterfaceHandle
    -- ^ Base interface handle
    , mcpRuntime :: MCPRuntime
    -- ^ MCP runtime state
    , mcpShutdown :: TVar Bool
    -- ^ Shutdown flag
    }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Initialization
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Initialize the MCP interface.
initMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceConfig -> IO MCPInterfaceHandle
initMCPInterface config = do
    -- Initialize OS world
    world <- atomically OSWorld.newWorld

    -- Create base interface components
    shutdownVar <- newTVarIO False
    agentsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    conversationsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    eventQueue <- newTQueueIO

    let baseHandle =
            InterfaceHandle
                { ihOS = world
                , ihConfig = mcpBaseConfig config
                , ihEventSub = eventQueue
                , ihShutdown = shutdownVar
                , ihAgents = agentsVar
                , ihConversations = conversationsVar
                }

    -- Initialize persistence if configured
    persistence <- case mcpPersistence config of
        MCPPersistence_None -> pure Nothing
        MCPPersistence_Sqlite path -> do
            handle <- OSPersistence.initSqlitePersistence path
            pure $ Just handle
        MCPPersistence_Custom _ -> pure Nothing -- Custom handled separately

    -- Create runtime
    toolsVar <- newTVarIO []
    sessionsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    pendingVar <- newTVarIO []

    let runtime =
            MCPRuntime
                { mcpRuntimeTools = toolsVar
                , mcpRuntimeSessions = sessionsVar
                , mcpRuntimePendingRequests = pendingVar
                , mcpRuntimePersistence = persistence
                , mcpRuntimeOnProgress = mcpOnSessionProgress config
                , mcpRuntimeTracer = mcpTracer config
                }

    -- Log initialization
    let tracer = mcpTracer config
    tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Initialize $ "MCP Server v" <> mcpServerVersion config

    pure $
        MCPInterfaceHandle
            { mcpBaseHandle = baseHandle
            , mcpRuntime = runtime
            , mcpShutdown = shutdownVar
            }

-- | Register a tool with the MCP interface.
registerTool :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> MappedTool -> IO ()
registerTool handle tool = do
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimeTools $ mcpRuntime handle) (tool :)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Server Operations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Run the MCP server (blocks until shutdown).
runMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> IO ()
runMCPInterface handle = do
    let runtime = mcpRuntime handle
    let tracer = mcpRuntimeTracer runtime

    -- Run JSON-RPC server
    let stack = runReaderT (runMcpStack $ mainMCP tracer) runtime
    stack `catch` \e -> do
        tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack $ "MCP Server error: " <> show (e :: SomeException)
        throwIO e
  where
    throwIO :: SomeException -> IO ()
    throwIO = Control.Exception.throwIO

-- | Main MCP handler loop.
mainMCP :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> McpStack ()
mainMCP tracer = do
    runJSONRPCT'
        Rpc.V2
        False
        (Conduit.sinkHandleFlush stdout)
        stdinC
        (handlerLoop tracer)

-- | Handler loop for MCP requests.
handlerLoop :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handlerLoop tracer = forever $ do
    mreq <- Rpc.receiveRequest
    case mreq of
        Nothing -> logDebugN "No request received"
        Just req -> do
            rt <- askRuntime
            asyncHandle <- liftIO $ async $ handleRequest tracer req
            liftIO $ runReaderT (runMcpStack $ addAsyncRequest req asyncHandle) rt

-- | Handle a single MCP request.
handleRequest :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.Request -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handleRequest tracer req = do
    let emsg = Rpc.fromRequest req :: Either Rpc.ErrorObj ClientMsg
    case emsg of
        Left err -> do
            liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack $ "Parse error: " <> show err
            Rpc.sendResponse $ Rpc.Response req.getReqVer (Aeson.toJSON err) req.getReqId
        Right msg -> handleParsedReq tracer req msg

-- | Client message types.
data ClientMsg
    = InitializeMsg InitializeRequest
    | NotifyInitializedMsg
    | ListResourcesRequestMsg ListResourcesRequest
    | ListToolsRequestMsg ListToolsRequest
    | ListPromptsRequestMsg ListPromptsRequest
    | CallToolRequestMsg CallToolRequest
    | CancelledNotificationMsg Mcp.CancelledNotification
    deriving (Show)

instance Rpc.FromRequest ClientMsg where
    parseParams "initialize" = Just (fmap InitializeMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "notifications/initialized" = Just (\_ -> pure NotifyInitializedMsg)
    parseParams "notifications/cancelled" =
        Just (fmap CancelledNotificationMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "resources/list" =
        Just (fmap ListResourcesRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "tools/list" = Just (fmap ListToolsRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "prompts/list" =
        Just (fmap ListPromptsRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "tools/call" = Just (fmap CallToolRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams _ = Nothing

-- | Handle parsed client message.
handleParsedReq :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.Request -> ClientMsg -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handleParsedReq _ req (InitializeMsg initReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    result <- liftIO $ handleInitialize rt initReq
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ _ NotifyInitializedMsg = pure () -- No response needed for notifications

handleParsedReq tracer req (ListToolsRequestMsg _) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    tools <- liftIO $ handleToolsList rt
    liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolsListed $ length tools
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req $ ListToolsResult tools Nothing

handleParsedReq tracer req (CallToolRequestMsg callReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    result <- liftIO $ handleToolCall rt callReq
    case result of
        Left err -> liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCompleted (callReq.name) (Left err)
        Right txt -> liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCompleted (callReq.name) (Right txt)
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req $ CallToolResult [toolCallContent result] Nothing

handleParsedReq _ req (ListResourcesRequestMsg _) = do
    result <- liftIO handleResourcesList
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ req (ListPromptsRequestMsg _) = do
    result <- liftIO handlePromptsList
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ _ (CancelledNotificationMsg cancelReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    liftIO $ runReaderT (runMcpStack $ cancelRequest cancelReq.requestId) rt

-- | Create JSON-RPC response.
respond :: (Aeson.ToJSON val) => Rpc.Request -> val -> Rpc.Response
respond req val = Rpc.Response req.getReqVer (Aeson.toJSON val) req.getReqId

-- | Convert tool result to MCP content.
toolCallContent :: Either String Text -> Content
toolCallContent (Left err) =
    TextContent $ TextContentImpl (Text.unwords ["Error:", Text.pack err]) (Just [])
toolCallContent (Right txt) =
    TextContent $ TextContentImpl txt (Just [])

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Handler Implementations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Handle initialize request.
handleInitialize :: MCPRuntime -> InitializeRequest -> IO InitializeResult
handleInitialize rt _req = do
    let caps =
            ServerCapabilities
                { experimental = Just mempty
                , logging = Just $ Aeson.object []
                , flags = [ToolsListChanged, PromptsListChanged, ResourcesListChanged]
                }
    pure $
        InitializeResult
            { protocolVersion = "2024-11-05"
            , capabilities = caps
            , serverInfo = Implementation (mcpServerName rt) (mcpServerVersion rt)
            , instructions = Just "Agents-exe MCP server exposing agents as tools"
            }
  where
    mcpServerName rt = "agents-exe-mcp-server"
    mcpServerVersion rt = "0.1.0"

-- | Handle tools/list request.
handleToolsList :: MCPRuntime -> IO [Tool]
handleToolsList rt = do
    mappedTools <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)
    pure $ map mappedToolToMCP mappedTools

-- | Convert mapped tool to MCP Tool.
mappedToolToMCP :: MappedTool -> Tool
mappedToolToMCP mappedTool =
    let tree = mappedToolTree mappedTool
        node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
        agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
        toolName = mappedToolName mappedTool
     in Tool
            { name = toolName
            , description = Just agentCfg.announce
            , inputSchema =
                Mcp.InputSchema
                    { required = Just ["prompt"]
                    , properties =
                        Just $
                            Aeson.fromList
                                [
                                    ( AesonKey.fromText "prompt"
                                    , Aeson.object
                                        [ "type" Aeson..= ("string" :: Text)
                                        , "description"
                                            Aeson..= ("The prompt to send to the agent" :: Text)
                                        ]
                                    )
                                ]
                    }
            }

-- | Handle tools/call request.
handleToolCall :: MCPRuntime -> CallToolRequest -> IO (Either String Text)
handleToolCall rt callReq = do
    mappedTools <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)
    case find (\t -> mappedToolName t == callReq.name) mappedTools of
        Nothing -> pure $ Left $ "Unknown tool: " <> Text.unpack callReq.name
        Just mappedTool -> runAgentTool rt mappedTool callReq

-- | Run agent with tool call.
runAgentTool :: MCPRuntime -> MappedTool -> CallToolRequest -> IO (Either String Text)
runAgentTool rt mappedTool callReq = do
    case extractPrompt callReq of
        Nothing -> pure $ Left "Missing 'prompt' argument"
        Just query -> do
            convId <- newConversationId
            let tracer = mcpRuntimeTracer rt
            tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCalled (mappedToolName mappedTool) (Text.pack $ show convId)

            result <-
                (Right <$> executeAgent mappedTool query convId rt)
                    `catch` (\e -> pure $ Left $ show (e :: SomeException))

            case result of
                Left err -> tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack err
                Right _ -> pure ()

            pure result

-- | Execute agent with query.
executeAgent :: MappedTool -> Text -> ConversationId -> MCPRuntime -> IO Text
executeAgent mappedTool query convId rt = do
    let tree = mappedToolTree mappedTool
    let node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
    let agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
    let apiKeys = mappedToolApiKeys mappedTool

    -- Get API key
    let mApiKey = lookupApiKey (AgentTree.apiKeyId agentCfg) apiKeys

    -- Create HTTP runtime
    httpRuntime <- case mApiKey of
        Just apiKey -> HttpClient.newRuntime (HttpClient.BearerToken $ Text.decodeUtf8 $ OpenAI.revealApiKey apiKey)
        Nothing -> HttpClient.newRuntime HttpClient.NoToken

    -- Create completion config
    let completionConfig =
            OpenAICompletionConfig
                { cfgTracer = Tracer $ \_ -> pure () -- silent
                , cfgRuntime = httpRuntime
                , cfgBaseUrl = OpenAI.ApiBaseUrl $ AgentTree.modelUrl agentCfg
                , cfgModelName = AgentTree.modelName agentCfg
                , cfgModelFlavor = parseModelFlavor $ AgentTree.flavor agentCfg
                }

    let completeF = mkOpenAICompletion completionConfig

    -- Read tools from TVar
    tools <- readTVarIO (AgentTree.osNodeTools node)
    let systemTools = map toolRegistrationToSystemTool tools

    let tp = ToolPortal.makeToolPortal (Tracer $ \_ -> pure ()) (AgentTree.osNodeTools node)

    -- Create agent
    let agent =
            Agent
                { step = naiveTilNoToolCallStep
                , sysPrompt = pure $ SystemPrompt $ Text.unlines $ AgentTree.systemPrompt agentCfg
                , sysTools = pure systemTools
                , usrQuery = pure $ Just $ UserQuery query
                , toolCall = executeLlmToolCall (Tracer $ \_ -> pure ()) (readTVarIO $ AgentTree.osNodeTools node) (SessionCompat.parseToolCallFromLlmToolCall, SessionCompat.callResultToUserToolResponse)
                , toolPortal = tp
                , complete = completeF
                , contextConfig = defaultContextConfig
                }

    -- Create session
    session0 <- Session [] <$> newSessionId <*> pure Nothing <*> newTurnId

    -- Notify progress
    mcpRuntimeOnProgress rt (SessionTypes.SessionStarted session0)

    -- Run agent
    (llmTurn, _) <- run convId agent session0

    -- Complete session
    mcpRuntimeOnProgress rt (SessionTypes.SessionCompleted session0)

    -- Persist if configured
    case mcpRuntimePersistence rt of
        Just handle -> OSPersistence.storeSession handle convId session0
        Nothing -> pure ()

    -- Extract response
    pure $ extractResponseText llmTurn.llmResponse

-- | Handle resources/list request.
handleResourcesList :: IO ListResourcesResult
handleResourcesList = pure $ ListResourcesResult [] Nothing

-- | Handle prompts/list request.
handlePromptsList :: IO ListPromptsResult
handlePromptsList = pure $ ListPromptsResult [] Nothing

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Utility Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Extract prompt from tool call arguments.
extractPrompt :: CallToolRequest -> Maybe Text
extractPrompt (CallToolRequest _ Nothing) = Nothing
extractPrompt (CallToolRequest _ (Just args)) =
    case Aeson.lookup "prompt" args of
        Just (Aeson.String txt) -> Just txt
        _ -> Nothing

-- | Look up API key by ID.
lookupApiKey :: Text -> AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys -> Maybe OpenAI.ApiKey
lookupApiKey keyId keys = fmap snd $ find ((== keyId) . fst) keys

-- | Parse model flavor from text.
parseModelFlavor :: Text -> OpenAI.ModelFlavor
parseModelFlavor txt = fromMaybe OpenAI.OpenAIv1 $ OpenAI.parseFlavor txt

-- | Convert ToolRegistration to SystemTool.
toolRegistrationToSystemTool :: ToolRegistration -> SessionTypes.SystemTool
toolRegistrationToSystemTool reg =
    let llmTool = reg.declareTool
        toolDef =
            SessionTypes.SystemToolDefinitionV1
                { name = llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                , llmName = llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                , description = llmTool.toolDescriptionText
                , properties = llmTool.toolDescriptionParamProperties
                , raw =
                    Aeson.object
                        [ "type" Aeson..= ("function" :: Text)
                        , "function"
                            Aeson..= Aeson.object
                                [ "name" Aeson..= llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                                , "description" Aeson..= llmTool.toolDescriptionText
                                , "parameters" Aeson..= toolParamsToJson llmTool.toolDescriptionParamProperties
                                ]
                        ]
                }
     in SessionTypes.SystemTool $ SessionTypes.V1 toolDef

-- | Convert tool parameters to JSON schema.
toolParamsToJson :: [ParamProperty] -> Aeson.Value
toolParamsToJson props =
    Aeson.object
        [ "type" Aeson..= ("object" :: Text)
        , "properties"
            Aeson..= Aeson.fromList (map paramPropertyToJson props)
        , "required" Aeson..= map propertyKey (filter propertyRequired props)
        , "additionalProperties" Aeson..= False
        ]
  where
    paramPropertyToJson p =
        ( AesonKey.fromText $ propertyKey p
        , paramTypeToJson p
        )
    paramTypeToJson p =
        Aeson.object $
            [ "type" Aeson..= paramTypeToString (propertyType p)
            , "description" Aeson..= propertyDescription p
            ]
                ++ case propertyType p of
                    EnumParamType enumVals -> ["enum" Aeson..= enumVals]
                    _ -> []

    paramTypeToString :: ParamType -> Text
    paramTypeToString NullParamType = "null"
    paramTypeToString StringParamType = "string"
    paramTypeToString BoolParamType = "boolean"
    paramTypeToString NumberParamType = "number"
    paramTypeToString (EnumParamType _) = "string"
    paramTypeToString (OpaqueParamType t) = t
    paramTypeToString (MultipleParamType t) = t
    paramTypeToString (ObjectParamType _) = "object"

-- | Extract response text from LLM response.
extractResponseText :: LlmResponse -> Text
extractResponseText (LlmResponse mtxt _ _ _) = fromMaybe "" mtxt

-- | Shutdown the MCP interface.
shutdownMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> IO ()
shutdownMCPInterface handle = do
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpShutdown handle) (const True)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- JSON-RPC Conduit
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Modified runJSONRPCT that flushes after each message.
runJSONRPCT' ::
    (MonadLoggerIO m, UnliftIO.MonadUnliftIO m) =>
    Rpc.Ver ->
    Bool ->
    Conduit.ConduitT (Conduit.Flush L8.ByteString) () m () ->
    Conduit.ConduitT () L8.ByteString m () ->
    Rpc.JSONRPCT m a ->
    m a
runJSONRPCT' ver ignore snk src f = do
    qs <- liftIO $ atomically $ Rpc.initSession ver ignore
    let inSnk = sinkTBMChan (Rpc.inCh qs)
        outSrc = sourceTBMChan (Rpc.outCh qs)
    UnliftIO.withAsync
        (Conduit.runConduit $ src .| Rpc.decodeConduit ver .| inSnk)
        $ \_ ->
            UnliftIO.withAsync
                (Conduit.runConduit $ outSrc .| encodeConduit .| snk)
                $ \o ->
                    UnliftIO.withAsync (runReaderT Rpc.processIncoming qs) $
                        \_ -> do
                            a <- runReaderT f qs
                            liftIO $ atomically $ closeTBMChan $ Rpc.outCh qs
                            _ <- UnliftIO.wait o
                            return a

encodeConduit :: (Aeson.ToJSON j, MonadLogger m) => Conduit.ConduitT j (Conduit.Flush L8.ByteString) m ()
encodeConduit = do
    Conduit.awaitForever $ \m -> do
        Conduit.yield $ Conduit.Chunk $ L8.toStrict $ Aeson.encode m
        Conduit.yield $ Conduit.Chunk "\n"
        Conduit.yield Conduit.Flush

sinkTBMChan :: TQueue a -> Conduit.ConduitT a Void IO ()
sinkTBMChan chan = Conduit.awaitForever $ liftIO . atomically . writeTQueue chan

sourceTBMChan :: TQueue a -> Conduit.ConduitT () a IO ()
sourceTBMChan chan = forever $ do
    msg <- liftIO $ atomically $ readTQueue chan
    Conduit.yield msg

closeTBMChan :: TQueue a -> STM ()
closeTBMChan _ = pure () -- STM channels don't need explicit closing

{"filepath": "src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces/MCP.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | OS-native MCP (Model Context Protocol) interface.

This module provides a complete MCP server implementation using OS-native
ECS primitives. It exposes agents as MCP tools and handles JSON-RPC 2.0
communication over stdin/stdout.

== Features

* Standard MCP protocol support (initialize, tools/list, tools/call)
* OS-native ECS integration for agent and conversation management
* Progressive disclosure support via dynamic tool visibility
* STM-based concurrency for thread-safe request handling
* Optional session persistence with SQLite backend
* Comprehensive event tracing

== Example Usage

@
import System.Agents.OS.Interfaces.MCP

-- Initialize MCP interface
config <- defaultMCPInterfaceConfig
handle <- initMCPInterface config

-- Run the MCP server (blocks until shutdown)
runMCPInterface handle
@
-}
module System.Agents.OS.Interfaces.MCP (
    -- * MCP Interface Handle
    MCPInterfaceHandle (..),
    MCPInterfaceConfig (..),
    MCPRuntime (..),

    -- * Initialization
    initMCPInterface,
    runMCPInterface,
    shutdownMCPInterface,

    -- * Server Operations
    handleInitialize,
    handleToolsList,
    handleToolCall,
    handlePromptsList,
    handleResourcesList,

    -- * Tool Mapping
    MappedTool (..),
    registerAgentAsTool,

    -- * Configuration
    defaultMCPInterfaceConfig,

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Tracing
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Trace events for MCP interface debugging.
data MCPTrace
    = MCPTrace_Initialize Text
    | MCPTrace_ToolsListed Int
    | MCPTrace_ToolCalled Text Text -- tool name, conversation id
    | MCPTrace_ToolCompleted Text (Either String Text)
    | MCPTrace_Error Text
    | MCPTrace_SessionProgress ConversationId Text
    deriving (Show)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Interface Configuration
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Persistence configuration for MCP sessions.
data MCPPersistence
    = -- | No session persistence
      MCPPersistence_None
    | -- | Persist to SQLite database
      MCPPersistence_Sqlite FilePath
    | -- | Custom persistence callback
      MCPPersistence_Custom (ConversationId -> Session -> IO ())
    deriving (Show)

-- | Configuration for the MCP interface.
data MCPInterfaceConfig = MCPInterfaceConfig
    { mcpBaseConfig :: InterfaceConfig
    -- ^ Base interface configuration
    , mcpProtocolVersion :: Text
    -- ^ MCP protocol version (default: "2024-11-05")
    , mcpServerName :: Text
    -- ^ Server name for initialize response
    , mcpServerVersion :: Text
    -- ^ Server version for initialize response
    , mcpPersistence :: MCPPersistence
    -- ^ Session persistence configuration
    , mcpOnSessionProgress :: OnSessionProgress
    -- ^ Callback for session progress tracking
    , mcpTracer :: Tracer IO MCPTrace
    -- ^ Tracer for debugging
    }

-- | Default MCP interface configuration.
defaultMCPInterfaceConfig :: IO MCPInterfaceConfig
defaultMCPInterfaceConfig = do
    let tracer = Tracer $ \_ -> pure () -- silent by default
    pure $
        MCPInterfaceConfig
            { mcpBaseConfig = (defaultInterfaceConfig){ifcMode = ModeMCP}
            , mcpProtocolVersion = "2024-11-05"
            , mcpServerName = "agents-exe-mcp-server"
            , mcpServerVersion = "0.1.0"
            , mcpPersistence = MCPPersistence_None
            , mcpOnSessionProgress = ignoreSessionProgress
            , mcpTracer = tracer
            }

-- | Operating mode (internal use).
data InterfaceMode = ModeMCP
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Mapped Tools
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | A mapped tool representing an expert agent exposed as an MCP tool.
Stores the OSAgentTree along with the API keys needed for execution.
-}
data MappedTool = MappedTool
    { mappedToolName :: Mcp.Name
    -- ^ MCP tool name (derived from agent slug)
    , mappedToolAgentId :: AgentId
    -- ^ OS entity ID for the agent
    , mappedToolTree :: AgentTree.OSAgentTree
    -- ^ The agent tree with configuration
    , mappedToolApiKeys :: AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys
    -- ^ API keys for LLM calls
    }
    deriving (Show)

type MappedTools = [MappedTool]

-- | Register an agent tree as an MCP tool.
registerAgentAsTool :: AgentTree.OSAgentTree -> AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys -> IO MappedTool
registerAgentAsTool tree apiKeys = do
    let node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
    let agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
    let toolName = agentCfg.slug
    agentId <- newAgentId
    pure $
        MappedTool
            { mappedToolName = toolName
            , mappedToolAgentId = agentId
            , mappedToolTree = tree
            , mappedToolApiKeys = apiKeys
            }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Runtime
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | MCP server runtime state.
data MCPRuntime = MCPRuntime
    { mcpRuntimeTools :: TVar MappedTools
    -- ^ Registered tools
    , mcpRuntimeSessions :: TVar (Map ConversationId Session)
    -- ^ Active sessions
    , mcpRuntimePendingRequests :: TVar [(Rpc.Request, Async ())]
    -- ^ Pending async requests for cancellation
    , mcpRuntimePersistence :: Maybe PersistenceHandle
    -- ^ Optional persistence handle
    , mcpRuntimeOnProgress :: OnSessionProgress
    -- ^ Session progress callback
    , mcpRuntimeTracer :: Tracer IO MCPTrace
    -- ^ Tracer for debugging
    }

-- | MCP server monad stack.
newtype McpStack a = McpStack {runMcpStack :: ReaderT MCPRuntime IO a}
    deriving (Functor, Applicative, Monad, MonadIO, MonadReader MCPRuntime)

instance MonadLogger McpStack where
    monadLoggerLog _ _ _ msg = do
        liftIO $ putStrLn $ Text.unpack msg

instance MonadLoggerIO McpStack where
    askLoggerIO = pure $ \_ _ _ msg -> putStrLn $ Text.unpack msg

-- | Get runtime from stack.
askRuntime :: Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack -> McpStack MCPRuntime
askRuntime _ = ask

-- | Get mapped tools.
askMappedTools :: McpStack MappedTools
askMappedTools = do
    rt <- ask
    readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)

-- | Add async request to pending list.
addAsyncRequest :: Rpc.Request -> Async () -> McpStack ()
addAsyncRequest req asyncHandle = do
    rt <- ask
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt) ((req, asyncHandle) :)

-- | Cancel pending request by ID.
cancelRequest :: Mcp.RequestId -> McpStack ()
cancelRequest reqId = do
    rt <- ask
    pending <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt)
    case find (matchingReqId reqId . fst) pending of
        Just (_, asyncHandle) -> do
            liftIO $ cancel asyncHandle
            atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimePendingRequests rt) (filter (not . matchingReqId reqId . fst))
        Nothing -> pure ()
  where
    matchingReqId :: Mcp.RequestId -> Rpc.Request -> Bool
    matchingReqId (Mcp.IntRequestId n) r = case r.getReqId of
        Rpc.IdInt m -> n == m
        _ -> False
    matchingReqId (Mcp.TextRequestId t) r = case r.getReqId of
        Rpc.IdTxt s -> t == s
        _ -> False

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- MCP Interface Handle
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Handle for MCP interface operations.
data MCPInterfaceHandle = MCPInterfaceHandle
    { mcpBaseHandle :: InterfaceHandle
    -- ^ Base interface handle
    , mcpRuntime :: MCPRuntime
    -- ^ MCP runtime state
    , mcpShutdown :: TVar Bool
    -- ^ Shutdown flag
    }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Initialization
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Initialize the MCP interface.
initMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceConfig -> IO MCPInterfaceHandle
initMCPInterface config = do
    -- Initialize OS world
    world <- atomically OSWorld.newWorld

    -- Create base interface components
    shutdownVar <- newTVarIO False
    agentsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    conversationsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    eventQueue <- newTQueueIO

    let baseHandle =
            InterfaceHandle
                { ihOS = world
                , ihConfig = mcpBaseConfig config
                , ihEventSub = eventQueue
                , ihShutdown = shutdownVar
                , ihAgents = agentsVar
                , ihConversations = conversationsVar
                }

    -- Initialize persistence if configured
    persistence <- case mcpPersistence config of
        MCPPersistence_None -> pure Nothing
        MCPPersistence_Sqlite path -> do
            handle <- OSPersistence.initSqlitePersistence path
            pure $ Just handle
        MCPPersistence_Custom _ -> pure Nothing -- Custom handled separately

    -- Create runtime
    toolsVar <- newTVarIO []
    sessionsVar <- newTVarIO Map.empty
    pendingVar <- newTVarIO []

    let runtime =
            MCPRuntime
                { mcpRuntimeTools = toolsVar
                , mcpRuntimeSessions = sessionsVar
                , mcpRuntimePendingRequests = pendingVar
                , mcpRuntimePersistence = persistence
                , mcpRuntimeOnProgress = mcpOnSessionProgress config
                , mcpRuntimeTracer = mcpTracer config
                }

    -- Log initialization
    let tracer = mcpTracer config
    tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Initialize $ "MCP Server v" <> mcpServerVersion config

    pure $
        MCPInterfaceHandle
            { mcpBaseHandle = baseHandle
            , mcpRuntime = runtime
            , mcpShutdown = shutdownVar
            }

-- | Register a tool with the MCP interface.
registerTool :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> MappedTool -> IO ()
registerTool handle tool = do
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpRuntimeTools $ mcpRuntime handle) (tool :)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Server Operations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Run the MCP server (blocks until shutdown).
runMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> IO ()
runMCPInterface handle = do
    let runtime = mcpRuntime handle
    let tracer = mcpRuntimeTracer runtime

    -- Run JSON-RPC server
    let stack = runReaderT (runMcpStack $ mainMCP tracer) runtime
    stack `catch` \e -> do
        tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack $ "MCP Server error: " <> show (e :: SomeException)
        throwIO e
  where
    throwIO :: SomeException -> IO ()
    throwIO = Control.Exception.throwIO

-- | Main MCP handler loop.
mainMCP :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> McpStack ()
mainMCP tracer = do
    runJSONRPCT'
        Rpc.V2
        False
        (Conduit.sinkHandleFlush stdout)
        stdinC
        (handlerLoop tracer)

-- | Handler loop for MCP requests.
handlerLoop :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handlerLoop tracer = forever $ do
    mreq <- Rpc.receiveRequest
    case mreq of
        Nothing -> logDebugN "No request received"
        Just req -> do
            rt <- askRuntime
            asyncHandle <- liftIO $ async $ handleRequest tracer req
            liftIO $ runReaderT (runMcpStack $ addAsyncRequest req asyncHandle) rt

-- | Handle a single MCP request.
handleRequest :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.Request -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handleRequest tracer req = do
    let emsg = Rpc.fromRequest req :: Either Rpc.ErrorObj ClientMsg
    case emsg of
        Left err -> do
            liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack $ "Parse error: " <> show err
            Rpc.sendResponse $ Rpc.Response req.getReqVer (Aeson.toJSON err) req.getReqId
        Right msg -> handleParsedReq tracer req msg

-- | Client message types.
data ClientMsg
    = InitializeMsg InitializeRequest
    | NotifyInitializedMsg
    | ListResourcesRequestMsg ListResourcesRequest
    | ListToolsRequestMsg ListToolsRequest
    | ListPromptsRequestMsg ListPromptsRequest
    | CallToolRequestMsg CallToolRequest
    | CancelledNotificationMsg Mcp.CancelledNotification
    deriving (Show)

instance Rpc.FromRequest ClientMsg where
    parseParams "initialize" = Just (fmap InitializeMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "notifications/initialized" = Just (\_ -> pure NotifyInitializedMsg)
    parseParams "notifications/cancelled" =
        Just (fmap CancelledNotificationMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "resources/list" =
        Just (fmap ListResourcesRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "tools/list" = Just (fmap ListToolsRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "prompts/list" =
        Just (fmap ListPromptsRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams "tools/call" = Just (fmap CallToolRequestMsg <$> Aeson.parseJSON)
    parseParams _ = Nothing

-- | Handle parsed client message.
handleParsedReq :: Tracer IO MCPTrace -> Rpc.Request -> ClientMsg -> Rpc.JSONRPCT McpStack ()
handleParsedReq _ req (InitializeMsg initReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    result <- liftIO $ handleInitialize rt initReq
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ _ NotifyInitializedMsg = pure () -- No response needed for notifications

handleParsedReq tracer req (ListToolsRequestMsg _) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    tools <- liftIO $ handleToolsList rt
    liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolsListed $ length tools
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req $ ListToolsResult tools Nothing

handleParsedReq tracer req (CallToolRequestMsg callReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    result <- liftIO $ handleToolCall rt callReq
    case result of
        Left err -> liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCompleted (callReq.name) (Left err)
        Right txt -> liftIO $ tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCompleted (callReq.name) (Right txt)
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req $ CallToolResult [toolCallContent result] Nothing

handleParsedReq _ req (ListResourcesRequestMsg _) = do
    result <- liftIO handleResourcesList
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ req (ListPromptsRequestMsg _) = do
    result <- liftIO handlePromptsList
    Rpc.sendResponse $ respond req result

handleParsedReq _ _ (CancelledNotificationMsg cancelReq) = do
    rt <- askRuntime
    liftIO $ runReaderT (runMcpStack $ cancelRequest cancelReq.requestId) rt

-- | Create JSON-RPC response.
respond :: (Aeson.ToJSON val) => Rpc.Request -> val -> Rpc.Response
respond req val = Rpc.Response req.getReqVer (Aeson.toJSON val) req.getReqId

-- | Convert tool result to MCP content.
toolCallContent :: Either String Text -> Content
toolCallContent (Left err) =
    TextContent $ TextContentImpl (Text.unwords ["Error:", Text.pack err]) (Just [])
toolCallContent (Right txt) =
    TextContent $ TextContentImpl txt (Just [])

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Handler Implementations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Handle initialize request.
handleInitialize :: MCPRuntime -> InitializeRequest -> IO InitializeResult
handleInitialize rt _req = do
    let caps =
            ServerCapabilities
                { experimental = Just mempty
                , logging = Just $ Aeson.object []
                , flags = [ToolsListChanged, PromptsListChanged, ResourcesListChanged]
                }
    pure $
        InitializeResult
            { protocolVersion = "2024-11-05"
            , capabilities = caps
            , serverInfo = Implementation (mcpServerName rt) (mcpServerVersion rt)
            , instructions = Just "Agents-exe MCP server exposing agents as tools"
            }
  where
    mcpServerName rt = "agents-exe-mcp-server"
    mcpServerVersion rt = "0.1.0"

-- | Handle tools/list request.
handleToolsList :: MCPRuntime -> IO [Tool]
handleToolsList rt = do
    mappedTools <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)
    pure $ map mappedToolToMCP mappedTools

-- | Convert mapped tool to MCP Tool.
mappedToolToMCP :: MappedTool -> Tool
mappedToolToMCP mappedTool =
    let tree = mappedToolTree mappedTool
        node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
        agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
        toolName = mappedToolName mappedTool
     in Tool
            { name = toolName
            , description = Just agentCfg.announce
            , inputSchema =
                Mcp.InputSchema
                    { required = Just ["prompt"]
                    , properties =
                        Just $
                            Aeson.fromList
                                [
                                    ( AesonKey.fromText "prompt"
                                    , Aeson.object
                                        [ "type" Aeson..= ("string" :: Text)
                                        , "description"
                                            Aeson..= ("The prompt to send to the agent" :: Text)
                                        ]
                                    )
                                ]
                    }
            }

-- | Handle tools/call request.
handleToolCall :: MCPRuntime -> CallToolRequest -> IO (Either String Text)
handleToolCall rt callReq = do
    mappedTools <- readTVarIO (mcpRuntimeTools rt)
    case find (\t -> mappedToolName t == callReq.name) mappedTools of
        Nothing -> pure $ Left $ "Unknown tool: " <> Text.unpack callReq.name
        Just mappedTool -> runAgentTool rt mappedTool callReq

-- | Run agent with tool call.
runAgentTool :: MCPRuntime -> MappedTool -> CallToolRequest -> IO (Either String Text)
runAgentTool rt mappedTool callReq = do
    case extractPrompt callReq of
        Nothing -> pure $ Left "Missing 'prompt' argument"
        Just query -> do
            convId <- newConversationId
            let tracer = mcpRuntimeTracer rt
            tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_ToolCalled (mappedToolName mappedTool) (Text.pack $ show convId)

            result <-
                (Right <$> executeAgent mappedTool query convId rt)
                    `catch` (\e -> pure $ Left $ show (e :: SomeException))

            case result of
                Left err -> tracer.emit $ MCPTrace_Error $ Text.pack err
                Right _ -> pure ()

            pure result

-- | Execute agent with query.
executeAgent :: MappedTool -> Text -> ConversationId -> MCPRuntime -> IO Text
executeAgent mappedTool query convId rt = do
    let tree = mappedToolTree mappedTool
    let node = AgentTree.osTreeRoot tree
    let agentCfg = AgentTree.osNodeConfig node
    let apiKeys = mappedToolApiKeys mappedTool

    -- Get API key
    let mApiKey = lookupApiKey (AgentTree.apiKeyId agentCfg) apiKeys

    -- Create HTTP runtime
    httpRuntime <- case mApiKey of
        Just apiKey -> HttpClient.newRuntime (HttpClient.BearerToken $ Text.decodeUtf8 $ OpenAI.revealApiKey apiKey)
        Nothing -> HttpClient.newRuntime HttpClient.NoToken

    -- Create completion config
    let completionConfig =
            OpenAICompletionConfig
                { cfgTracer = Tracer $ \_ -> pure () -- silent
                , cfgRuntime = httpRuntime
                , cfgBaseUrl = OpenAI.ApiBaseUrl $ AgentTree.modelUrl agentCfg
                , cfgModelName = AgentTree.modelName agentCfg
                , cfgModelFlavor = parseModelFlavor $ AgentTree.flavor agentCfg
                }

    let completeF = mkOpenAICompletion completionConfig

    -- Read tools from TVar
    tools <- readTVarIO (AgentTree.osNodeTools node)
    let systemTools = map toolRegistrationToSystemTool tools

    let tp = ToolPortal.makeToolPortal (Tracer $ \_ -> pure ()) (AgentTree.osNodeTools node)

    -- Create agent
    let agent =
            Agent
                { step = naiveTilNoToolCallStep
                , sysPrompt = pure $ SystemPrompt $ Text.unlines $ AgentTree.systemPrompt agentCfg
                , sysTools = pure systemTools
                , usrQuery = pure $ Just $ UserQuery query
                , toolCall = executeLlmToolCall (Tracer $ \_ -> pure ()) (readTVarIO $ AgentTree.osNodeTools node) (SessionCompat.parseToolCallFromLlmToolCall, SessionCompat.callResultToUserToolResponse)
                , toolPortal = tp
                , complete = completeF
                , contextConfig = defaultContextConfig
                }

    -- Create session
    session0 <- Session [] <$> newSessionId <*> pure Nothing <*> newTurnId

    -- Notify progress
    mcpRuntimeOnProgress rt (SessionTypes.SessionStarted session0)

    -- Run agent
    (llmTurn, _) <- run convId agent session0

    -- Complete session
    mcpRuntimeOnProgress rt (SessionTypes.SessionCompleted session0)

    -- Persist if configured
    case mcpRuntimePersistence rt of
        Just handle -> OSPersistence.storeSession handle convId session0
        Nothing -> pure ()

    -- Extract response
    pure $ extractResponseText llmTurn.llmResponse

-- | Handle resources/list request.
handleResourcesList :: IO ListResourcesResult
handleResourcesList = pure $ ListResourcesResult [] Nothing

-- | Handle prompts/list request.
handlePromptsList :: IO ListPromptsResult
handlePromptsList = pure $ ListPromptsResult [] Nothing

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Utility Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Extract prompt from tool call arguments.
extractPrompt :: CallToolRequest -> Maybe Text
extractPrompt (CallToolRequest _ Nothing) = Nothing
extractPrompt (CallToolRequest _ (Just args)) =
    case Aeson.lookup "prompt" args of
        Just (Aeson.String txt) -> Just txt
        _ -> Nothing

-- | Look up API key by ID.
lookupApiKey :: Text -> AgentTree.LoadedApiKeys -> Maybe OpenAI.ApiKey
lookupApiKey keyId keys = fmap snd $ find ((== keyId) . fst) keys

-- | Parse model flavor from text.
parseModelFlavor :: Text -> OpenAI.ModelFlavor
parseModelFlavor txt = fromMaybe OpenAI.OpenAIv1 $ OpenAI.parseFlavor txt

-- | Convert ToolRegistration to SystemTool.
toolRegistrationToSystemTool :: ToolRegistration -> SessionTypes.SystemTool
toolRegistrationToSystemTool reg =
    let llmTool = reg.declareTool
        toolDef =
            SessionTypes.SystemToolDefinitionV1
                { name = llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                , llmName = llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                , description = llmTool.toolDescriptionText
                , properties = llmTool.toolDescriptionParamProperties
                , raw =
                    Aeson.object
                        [ "type" Aeson..= ("function" :: Text)
                        , "function"
                            Aeson..= Aeson.object
                                [ "name" Aeson..= llmTool.toolDescriptionName.getToolName
                                , "description" Aeson..= llmTool.toolDescriptionText
                                , "parameters" Aeson..= toolParamsToJson llmTool.toolDescriptionParamProperties
                                ]
                        ]
                }
     in SessionTypes.SystemTool $ SessionTypes.V1 toolDef

-- | Convert tool parameters to JSON schema.
toolParamsToJson :: [ParamProperty] -> Aeson.Value
toolParamsToJson props =
    Aeson.object
        [ "type" Aeson..= ("object" :: Text)
        , "properties"
            Aeson..= Aeson.fromList (map paramPropertyToJson props)
        , "required" Aeson..= map propertyKey (filter propertyRequired props)
        , "additionalProperties" Aeson..= False
        ]
  where
    paramPropertyToJson p =
        ( AesonKey.fromText $ propertyKey p
        , paramTypeToJson p
        )
    paramTypeToJson p =
        Aeson.object $
            [ "type" Aeson..= paramTypeToString (propertyType p)
            , "description" Aeson..= propertyDescription p
            ]
                ++ case propertyType p of
                    EnumParamType enumVals -> ["enum" Aeson..= enumVals]
                    _ -> []

    paramTypeToString :: ParamType -> Text
    paramTypeToString NullParamType = "null"
    paramTypeToString StringParamType = "string"
    paramTypeToString BoolParamType = "boolean"
    paramTypeToString NumberParamType = "number"
    paramTypeToString (EnumParamType _) = "string"
    paramTypeToString (OpaqueParamType t) = t
    paramTypeToString (MultipleParamType t) = t
    paramTypeToString (ObjectParamType _) = "object"

-- | Extract response text from LLM response.
extractResponseText :: LlmResponse -> Text
extractResponseText (LlmResponse mtxt _ _ _) = fromMaybe "" mtxt

-- | Shutdown the MCP interface.
shutdownMCPInterface :: MCPInterfaceHandle -> IO ()
shutdownMCPInterface handle = do
    atomically $ modifyTVar' (mcpShutdown handle) (const True)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- JSON-RPC Conduit
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Modified runJSONRPCT that flushes after each message.
runJSONRPCT' ::
    (MonadLoggerIO m, UnliftIO.MonadUnliftIO m) =>
    Rpc.Ver ->
    Bool ->
    Conduit.ConduitT (Conduit.Flush L8.ByteString) () m () ->
    Conduit.ConduitT () L8.ByteString m () ->
    Rpc.JSONRPCT m a ->
    m a
runJSONRPCT' ver ignore snk src f = do
    qs <- liftIO $ atomically $ Rpc.initSession ver ignore
    let inSnk = sinkTBMChan (Rpc.inCh qs)
        outSrc = sourceTBMChan (Rpc.outCh qs)
    UnliftIO.withAsync
        (Conduit.runConduit $ src .| Rpc.decodeConduit ver .| inSnk)
        $ \_ ->
            UnliftIO.withAsync
                (Conduit.runConduit $ outSrc .| encodeConduit .| snk)
                $ \o ->
                    UnliftIO.withAsync (runReaderT Rpc.processIncoming qs) $
                        \_ -> do
                            a <- runReaderT f qs
                            liftIO $ atomically $ closeTBMChan $ Rpc.outCh qs
                            _ <- UnliftIO.wait o
                            return a

encodeConduit :: (Aeson.ToJSON j, MonadLogger m) => Conduit.ConduitT j (Conduit.Flush L8.ByteString) m ()
encodeConduit = do
    Conduit.awaitForever $ \m -> do
        Conduit.yield $ Conduit.Chunk $ L8.toStrict $ Aeson.encode m
        Conduit.yield $ Conduit.Chunk "\n"
        Conduit.yield Conduit.Flush

sinkTBMChan :: TQueue a -> Conduit.ConduitT a Void IO ()
sinkTBMChan chan = Conduit.awaitForever $ liftIO . atomically . writeTQueue chan

sourceTBMChan :: TQueue a -> Conduit.ConduitT () a IO ()
sourceTBMChan chan = forever $ do
    msg <- liftIO $ atomically $ readTQueue chan
    Conduit.yield msg

closeTBMChan :: TQueue a -> STM ()
closeTBMChan _ = pure () -- STM channels don't need explicit closing
